events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: residential job, conductor selection & sizing, observed field condition: wrong insulation type for wet location. Work rough-complete. Reference NEC 310, Table 310.16. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Wrong insulation type for wet location is non-compliant and likely installed-defective. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Risk? Wet location wrong insulation can be high/critical. Need choose. Residential service call, wrong insulation type for wet location. NEC requires conductors in wet/damp locations have insulation suitable for wet location (e.g., THWN-2, XHHW-2, USE-2 etc). Table 310.16 ampacity based on insulation temp rating; if wrong insulation type, may not be rated for wet location and may overheat/short. Risk high maybe critical if energized in wet location. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 75? Value not asked 0. Sound fields irrelevant 0. Findings: conductor installed has insulation type not rated for wet location; violates NEC 310.4? Actually NEC 310.4? Need references: NEC 310.4? Let's recall NEC 2020: Article 310 covers conductors for general wiring. Section 310.4? Conductors. Section 310.16 ampacities for not more than three current-carrying conductors in raceway/cable. Wet location requirement in 310.10(C) maybe? Actually 310.10(C) Conductors in Wet Locations. In NEC 2014/2017: 310.10(C) says insulated conductors used in wet locations shall be of a type listed for use in wet locations, and the conductor insulation, where used, shall be of a type suitable for use in wet locations. Also 110.3(B) installation per listing. Table 310.16 used for ampacity. So references: NEC 310.10(C), Table 310.16. Need actions: replace conductors with insulation type rated for wet location; verify ampacity per Table 310.16; inspect/re-test. Rationale brief. Need output exactly JSON. Ensure no prose outside.
